natural_language: Six blocks, two independent three-block towers sitting side by side. Tower number one from bottom: A on table, B on A, C on top of B. Tower number two from bottom: D on table, E on D, F at the peak. The only reachable blocks initially are C (left tower's peak) and F (right tower's peak). The hand begins free. For the goal, each tower needs its top two blocks swapped while the bottom block stays put. So tower one should become: A still on the table, then C on A (note: C moves to the middle), and B on C at the peak. Tower two similarly: D on table, then F on D, and E sits on F at the peak. Two independent swaps, nothing crosses between towers.
